product_type: A Non-linked Non-participating Individual Life Insurance Savings Plan
summary: Shriram Life Sunishchit Laabh is a non-linked, non-participating individual life insurance savings plan designed to provide financial security and guaranteed long-term income. It combines life insurance with guaranteed income payouts for up to 40 years, offering two income payout options (Income Plus, Income Pro), two life cover options (Life, Life Plus), loyalty additions, and flexibility in choosing terms to meet financial goals and secure a legacy.

quick_facts.sum_insured: Minimum: ₹ 2,10,000 (with Death Sum Assured option of 7 x Annualized Premium). Maximum: No limit, subject to Board approved underwriting policy.
quick_facts.policy_term: For Premium Paying Term (PPT) 7 to 10 years, the Policy Term (PT) is 11 years. For PPT 15 to 25 years, the PT is PPT+1 year.
quick_facts.waiting_period: For minor lives, risk cover starts from the 1st policy anniversary. In case of death during the first policy year, total premiums paid will be refunded. Death due to suicide within 12 months from commencement or revival has a specific clause.

coverage: Death Benefit: Pays a 'Death Sum Assured' to the nominee in case of the life assured's death during the policy term. The Death Sum Assured is the higher of 10 or 7 times the annualised premium, 105% of total premiums paid, or the surrender value.
Life Cover Option 'Life': Pays the Death Sum Assured in a lump sum immediately upon death during the policy term.

Life Cover Option 'Life Plus': Pays the Death Sum Assured in equal monthly instalments until the end of the policy term. Future premiums are waived, and maturity benefits continue to be paid to the family.
Maturity Benefit: Paid upon survival till the end of the policy term, as per the chosen payout option (Income Plus or Income Pro).

Optional Riders: Enhanced protection through 5 optional riders: Accident Benefit Rider, Family Income Benefit Rider, Shriram Life Critical Illness Plus Rider, Shriram Extra Insurance Cover Rider, and Shriram Life Critical Illness Woman Rider.
benefits: Guaranteed Returns: Offers guaranteed annual income throughout the payout period and guaranteed returns up to 605% of Total Premiums Paid (TPP).
Maturity Payout Options: Choice between 'Income Plus' (level guaranteed income) and 'Income Pro' (guaranteed income plus a refund of all premiums paid at the end of the payout period).
Loyalty Additions: Additional percentage of the annualised premium added to the guaranteed maturity income.
Flexibility: Option to choose premium paying terms (7 to 25 years), policy terms, and income payout periods (10 to 40 years).
Female Life Incentives: Higher maturity benefit payout for female lives.
High Premium Incentives: Increased maturity factor for policies with higher annual premiums.
Loan Facility: Option to avail a loan against the policy after the first full year's premium has been paid.
Tax Benefits: Tax-free returns as per extant tax laws.
eligibility: Age at Entry: Minimum 30 days, Maximum 60 years (age last birthday).
Maturity Age: Minimum 18 years, Maximum 76 years (age last birthday).
Premium Paying Term (PPT): 7 to 10 years (in multiples of 1 year) or 15 to 25 years (in multiples of 5 years).
Policy Term (PT): For PPT 7-10 years, PT is 11 years. For PPT 15-25 years, PT is PPT+1 year.
Maturity Benefit Payout Term: 10 to 40 years (in multiples of 5 years), up to a maximum age of 101 years.
Premium Payment Mode: Yearly, Half Yearly, Quarterly, Monthly.
Minimum Premium: Yearly: ₹30,000, Half-Yearly: ₹15,500, Quarterly: ₹8,000, Monthly: ₹3,000.
Sum Assured Variants: 7 times or 10 times of annualised premium.
exclusions: Suicide Exclusion: If the life assured commits suicide within 12 months from the date of commencement of risk or date of revival, the nominee will be entitled to 80% of the total premiums paid till the date of death or the surrender value, whichever is higher, provided the policy is in force.
claims_information: Grievance Redressal Officer Contact: Phone: 040-23009400, Email: grievance.redressal@shriramlife.in.
General Customer Care: Toll-Free Number: 1800-103-6116, Email: customercare@shriramlife.in.

Fraud or Misstatement: Action will be initiated as per Section 45 of the Insurance Act, 1938. A policy can be called into question within three years from the date of issuance, commencement of risk, or revival on grounds of fraud or misstatement.

faqs.question: What is Shriram Life Sunishchit Laabh?
faqs.answer: It is a non-linked, non-participating individual life insurance savings plan that provides financial security with guaranteed long-term income and life cover.

faqs.question: Who is the insurer for this plan?
faqs.answer: The insurer is Shriram Life Insurance Company Limited.

faqs.question: What are the main benefits of this plan?
faqs.answer: Key benefits include guaranteed annual income, an option for a refund of all premiums paid at the end of the income payout period, life cover, and tax-free returns under extant tax laws.

faqs.question: What are the life cover options available?
faqs.answer: There are two options: 'Life' and 'Life Plus' (with an in-built Waiver of Premium).

faqs.question: What is the difference between the 'Life' and 'Life Plus' options?
faqs.answer: Under the 'Life' option, the death benefit is paid as a lump sum. Under the 'Life Plus' option, the death sum assured is paid in monthly instalments, future premiums are waived, and maturity benefits are still paid out as scheduled.

faqs.question: What are the maturity benefit payout options?
faqs.answer: There are two options: 'Income Plus', which provides a level guaranteed income, and 'Income Pro', which provides a guaranteed income and returns the total premiums paid at the end of the payout period.

faqs.question: What is the minimum and maximum entry age for this policy?
faqs.answer: The minimum entry age is 30 days and the maximum is 60 years (age last birthday).

faqs.question: What are the available Premium Paying Terms (PPT)?
faqs.answer: You can choose a PPT from 7 to 10 years (in yearly multiples) or from 15 to 25 years (in 5-year multiples).

faqs.question: What is the Policy Term (PT)?
faqs.answer: For a PPT of 7 to 10 years, the PT is 11 years. For a PPT of 15 to 25 years, the PT is the PPT plus one year.

faqs.question: For how long can I receive the income payouts?
faqs.answer: You can choose an income payout period from 10 to 40 years, in multiples of 5 years.

faqs.question: What happens if the insured person dies during the policy term?
faqs.answer: The Death Sum Assured is paid to the nominee. The payout method depends on whether the 'Life' (lump sum) or 'Life Plus' (monthly instalments with waiver of premium) option was chosen.

faqs.question: What if the insured person dies after the policy term but during the income payout period?
faqs.answer: The guaranteed maturity income and loyalty additions will continue to be paid to the nominee for the remainder of the chosen payout term.

faqs.question: What are Loyalty Additions?
faqs.answer: Loyalty Additions are an additional percentage of the annualised premium that are added to the Guaranteed Maturity Income at the end of the policy term. The percentage depends on the premium payment term.

faqs.question: Are there any special benefits for female policyholders?
faqs.answer: Yes, female lives are eligible to receive a higher maturity benefit payout compared to a similar male life.

faqs.question: Is there an incentive for paying a higher premium?
faqs.answer: Yes, the plan offers a high premium incentive by increasing the maturity factor for annual premiums of ₹60,000 and above.

faqs.question: What payment modes are available for the premium?
faqs.answer: Premiums can be paid Yearly, Half-yearly, Quarterly, or Monthly.

faqs.question: What is the grace period for paying the premium?
faqs.answer: A 30-day grace period is allowed for yearly, half-yearly, and quarterly modes, and a 15-day grace period for the monthly mode.

faqs.question: What happens if I stop paying premiums?
faqs.answer: If premiums for at least one full year have been paid, the policy converts to a 'reduced paid-up' status with proportionately reduced benefits. If not, the policy will lapse and no benefits will be paid.

faqs.question: Can I revive a lapsed or paid-up policy?
faqs.answer: Yes, a policy can be revived within a revival period of five years from the date of the first unpaid premium by paying all outstanding premiums with interest.

faqs.question: Can I take a loan against this policy?
faqs.answer: Yes, a loan can be availed after paying premiums for at least one full year. The maximum loan amount is up to 90% of the surrender value.

faqs.question: Are there any optional riders available with this plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are five optional riders for enhanced protection: Accident Benefit Rider, Family Income Benefit Rider, Critical Illness Plus Rider, Extra Insurance Cover Rider, and Critical Illness Woman Rider.

faqs.question: What is the Suicide Exclusion clause?
faqs.answer: If death is due to suicide within 12 months of policy commencement or revival, the nominee receives 80% of total premiums paid or the surrender value, whichever is higher.

faqs.question: Are the returns from this policy tax-free?
faqs.answer: The brochure states that returns are tax-free as per extant tax laws. It is advisable to consult a tax advisor for details.

faqs.question: Is there a free-look period for this policy?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a 30-day free-look period from the date of receipt of the policy document to review its terms and conditions.

faqs.question: What is the rule for a minor life assured?
faqs.answer: For minor lives (below 18 years), the risk cover starts from the 1st policy anniversary. If the minor dies during the first policy year, the total premiums paid will be refunded.

faqs.question: How is the 'Death Sum Assured' calculated?
faqs.answer: The 'Death Sum Assured' is defined as the highest of: 10 or 7 times the annualised premium (as chosen), 105% of the Total Premiums Paid till the date of death, or the Surrender Value on the date of death.

faqs.question: Can I change my maturity benefit payout mode after buying the policy?
faqs.answer: You can change the frequency of receiving maturity benefit payouts (e.g., from yearly to monthly). However, you cannot change the Maturity Income Option (Income Plus/Income Pro) or the Payout Term opted at the time of proposal.
